task_completion.description: Log in to the Rancher dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser showing the Rancher login screen at https://localhost followed by the authenticated Rancher dashboard.
Rancher UI showing the cluster explorer or workloads page filtered by or displaying the 'ml-workloads' namespace.
Rancher UI displaying the 'ml-inference' deployment details, showing pods in an 'Evicted' state or events indicating ephemeral storage issues.

task_completion.description: Edit the deployment to resolve the ephemeral storage eviction
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the edit screen for the 'ml-inference' deployment (either form view or YAML view) where ephemeral storage limits/requests are being increased or removed.

task_completion.description: Verify both replicas reach a Running state
task_completion.visual_evidence: Rancher UI showing the 'ml-inference' deployment with 2/2 pods in a 'Running' state (typically indicated by green status icons).

integrity.description: Agent used the Rancher GUI to resolve the issue
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the Rancher web interface in a browser to edit the deployment, rather than exclusively using kubectl commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with a real web browser
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows https://localhost and the Rancher UI is genuinely rendered, not a text editor or mocked interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not fake the final state using browser developer tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The final 'Running' state is shown in the standard Rancher UI without the browser developer tools (Inspect Element) open to manipulate the DOM.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'ml-workloads'. Target deployment: 'ml-inference'. The agent must resolve the Kubelet pod eviction issue (typically by adjusting ephemeral storage limits/requests) so that exactly 2 replicas reach the 'Running' state.
